{{神社 |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''堅田神社'''（かただじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]]{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}（内宮）の[[摂末社|摂社]]。内宮の摂社27社のうち第15位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ。 [[二見浦]]が内宮と[[Toyouke Daijingu|外宮]]の両宮に納める御塩が生産される契機となった神社であり、土地神を祀る辰巳出版（2013）：77ページ。 == 概要 == [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2に鎮座する{{#tag:ref|出典では「二見町江堅田442-2」と記載。旧[[度会郡]][[二見町 (三重県)|二見町]]大字江字堅田は旧伊勢市などとの合併により、伊勢市二見町茶屋字堅田に変更となった{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=伊勢市市民交流課|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}。|group="注"}}。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』の「榎村神社」・「大国玉比売神社」双方の論社となっている。堅田神社を「大国玉比売神社」に比定する見解は、度会延経や[[伴信友]]が述べており、薗田守良は堅田神社の西方に「大国玉比売神社」があったのではないか、と述べている式内社研究会 編（1990）：253ページ。ほかに{{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}が「大国玉比売神社」の論社となっている。 社地の[[面積]]は2反5畝11歩（≒2,516[[平方メートル|m2]]）宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：10ページ。[[神体]]は石であり、[[社殿]]は長さ4.4尺（≒1.3m）×広さ5尺（≒1.5m）×高さ6.5尺（≒2.0m）である福山ほか（1975）：150ページ。 == 祭神 == [[祭神]]は佐見都日女命（さみつひめのみこと）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：95ページ学研パブリッシング（2013）：70ページ。土地の[[神 (神道)|神]]とされる。式内社「大国玉比売神社」の後継が堅田神社であると考えると、佐見都日女命は[[伊勢国]]の大国玉、すなわち[[伊勢津彦]]の娘となる。「佐見」とは鎮座地の古い[[地名]]である。 == 歴史 == [[倭姫命]]が諸国を巡幸し二見の地を訪れた際に、土地の神である佐見都日女命は倭姫命に堅塩を奉った。倭姫命はこれを喜び、堅田神社を定めた。ただし、佐見都日女命は倭姫命に進んで堅塩を奉じたわけではなく、倭姫命の質問に一切答えず、黙って堅塩を奉じることで、自らの開拓した土地を差し出すことにせめてもの抵抗を示したのである。これが二見浦で内宮・外宮へ納める御塩を生産する起源となった。 堅田神社は一時祭祀が中断したものの、荒木田氏経が[[永享]]6年（[[1434年]]）に再興し、[[寛文]]10年（[[1670年]]）に二見が[[寺社領|神領]]に復すると、[[江戸幕府]]は領民に[[祭礼]]と[[遷宮]]を命じ、こぞって奉仕したという{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=コース05 二見ルート〜禊と塩のまち二見の神社・夫婦岩・蘇民の森〜|publisher=伊勢市観光協会|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}}。 [[1921年]]（大正10年）3月に建て替えが行われている。 == 周辺 == 音無山の西麓、伊勢市役所二見総合支所の向かいに鎮座する。付近には二見旅館街があり、旅館街を抜けると[[二見興玉神社]]（[[夫婦岩]]）に到達する。 [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]][[二見浦駅]]から徒歩約6分（約400m）{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=13 御塩道|publisher=横山ビジターセンター|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}。二見総合支所前バス停からすぐ。[[自家用車]]利用の場合、[[伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン]][[二見ジャンクション|二見JCT]]より[[国道42号]]を経由、約1分（約1.2km）である。 ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50年4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Jingu Shrines|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}} == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|かたたしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:堅田神社]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:nnn]]

{{神社 |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Katada Shrine''' (堅田神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} (sessha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It ranks 15th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 It is the shrine that initiated the production of sacred salt (御塩, goshio) offered to both the Inner and {{ill|Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja|外宮}} at {{ill|Futamiura|ja|二見浦}}, enshrining the local deity.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 77 == Overview == Katada Shrine is located at 442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.{{#tag:ref|The source notes the address as "442-2 Katada, Aza E, Futami-cho," but due to mergers with the former Ise City and other municipalities, it was changed to "442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, Ise City." {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=Ise City Citizen Exchange Division|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}|group="Note"}} It is considered one of the candidate sites (論社) for both the "Enomura Shrine" and "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The view that identifies Katada Shrine as "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" was expressed by Watari Nobunori and {{ill|Tomonobu Ban|ja|伴信友}}, while Sonoda Moriyoshi speculated that "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" might have been located west of Katada Shrine.Edited by Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 253 {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}} is also considered a candidate site for "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine." The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 2,516 square meters (2 tan, 5 se, 11 bu).Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}} (sacred object) is a stone, and the {{ill|honden|ja|社殿}} (main hall) measures approximately 1.3 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and 2.0 meters in height.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|ja|伊勢津彦}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|ja|夫婦岩}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|ja|二見浦駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} of {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路}}. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:堅田神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} ; 注釈 {{Reflist|group="注"}} ; 出典 {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50年4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}} == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|かたたしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:堅田神社]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:nnn]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Untranslated pages]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Katada Shrine''' (堅田神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} (sessha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It ranks 15th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 It is the shrine that initiated the production of sacred salt (御塩, goshio) offered to both the Inner and {{ill|Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja|外宮}} at {{ill|Futamiura|ja|二見浦}}, enshrining the local deity.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 77 == Overview == Katada Shrine is located at 442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.{{#tag:ref|The source notes the address as "442-2 Katada, Aza E, Futami-cho," but due to mergers with the former Ise City and other municipalities, it was changed to "442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, Ise City." {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=Ise City Citizen Exchange Division|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}|group="Note"}} It is considered one of the candidate sites (論社) for both the "Enomura Shrine" and "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The view that identifies Katada Shrine as "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" was expressed by Watari Nobunori and {{ill|Tomonobu Ban|ja|伴信友}}, while Sonoda Moriyoshi speculated that "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" might have been located west of Katada Shrine.Edited by Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 253 {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}} is also considered a candidate site for "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine." The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 2,516 square meters (2 tan, 5 se, 11 bu).Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}} (sacred object) is a stone, and the {{ill|honden|ja|社殿}} (main hall) measures approximately 1.3 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and 2.0 meters in height.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|ja|伊勢津彦}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|ja|夫婦岩}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|ja|二見浦駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} of {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路}}. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50年4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}} == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|かたたしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:nnn]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Katada Shrine''' (堅田神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} (sessha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It ranks 15th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 It is the shrine that initiated the production of sacred salt (御塩, goshio) offered to both the Inner and {{ill|Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja|外宮}} at {{ill|Futamiura|ja|二見浦}}, enshrining the local deity.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 77 == Overview == Katada Shrine is located at 442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.{{#tag:ref|The source notes the address as "442-2 Katada, Aza E, Futami-cho," but due to mergers with the former Ise City and other municipalities, it was changed to "442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, Ise City." {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=Ise City Citizen Exchange Division|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}|group="Note"}} It is considered one of the candidate sites (論社) for both the "Enomura Shrine" and "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The view that identifies Katada Shrine as "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" was expressed by Watari Nobunori and {{ill|Tomonobu Ban|ja|伴信友}}, while Sonoda Moriyoshi speculated that "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" might have been located west of Katada Shrine.Edited by Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 253 {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}} is also considered a candidate site for "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine." The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 2,516 square meters (2 tan, 5 se, 11 bu).Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}} (sacred object) is a stone, and the {{ill|honden|ja|社殿}} (main hall) measures approximately 1.3 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and 2.0 meters in height.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|ja|伊勢津彦}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|ja|夫婦岩}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|ja|二見浦駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} of {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路}}. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:堅田神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} ; 注釈 {{Reflist|group="注"}} ; 出典 {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50年4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}} == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|かたたしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Katada Shrine-qq]] [[ja:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:nnn]]

By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路}}. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:堅田神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} ; 注釈 {{Reflist|group="注"}} ; 出典 {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50年4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}} == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|かたたしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katada Shrine-qq]] [[ja:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Subst:MAINSPACE]] to [[Category:q-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}-q]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Katada Shrine''' (堅田神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} (sessha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It ranks 15th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 It is the shrine that initiated the production of sacred salt (御塩, goshio) offered to both the Inner and {{ill|Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja|外宮}} at {{ill|Futamiura|ja|二見浦}}, enshrining the local deity.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 77 == Overview == Katada Shrine is located at 442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.{{#tag:ref|The source notes the address as "442-2 Katada, Aza E, Futami-cho," but due to mergers with the former Ise City and other municipalities, it was changed to "442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, Ise City." {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=Ise City Citizen Exchange Division|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}|group="Note"}} It is considered one of the candidate sites (論社) for both the "Enomura Shrine" and "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The view that identifies Katada Shrine as "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" was expressed by Watari Nobunori and {{ill|Tomonobu Ban|ja|伴信友}}, while Sonoda Moriyoshi speculated that "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" might have been located west of Katada Shrine.Edited by Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 253 {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}} is also considered a candidate site for "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine." The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 2,516 square meters (2 tan, 5 se, 11 bu).Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}} (sacred object) is a stone, and the {{ill|honden|ja|社殿}} (main hall) measures approximately 1.3 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and 2.0 meters in height.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|ja|伊勢津彦}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|ja|夫婦岩}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|ja|二見浦駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} of {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路}}. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:堅田神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} ; 注釈 {{Reflist|group="注"}} ; 出典 {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50年4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}} == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|かたたしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katada Shrine-qq]] [[ja:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]]

{{神社 |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Katada Shrine''' (堅田神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} (sessha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It ranks 15th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 It is the shrine that initiated the production of sacred salt (御塩, goshio) offered to both the Inner and {{ill|Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja|外宮}} at {{ill|Futamiura|ja|二見浦}}, enshrining the local deity.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 77 == Overview == Katada Shrine is located at 442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.{{#tag:ref|The source notes the address as "442-2 Katada, Aza E, Futami-cho," but due to mergers with the former Ise City and other municipalities, it was changed to "442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, Ise City." {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=Ise City Citizen Exchange Division|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}|group="Note"}} It is considered one of the candidate sites (論社) for both the "Enomura Shrine" and "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The view that identifies Katada Shrine as "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" was expressed by Watari Nobunori and {{ill|Tomonobu Ban|ja|伴信友}}, while Sonoda Moriyoshi speculated that "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" might have been located west of Katada Shrine.Edited by Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 253 {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}} is also considered a candidate site for "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine." The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 2,516 square meters (2 tan, 5 se, 11 bu).Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}} (sacred object) is a stone, and the {{ill|honden|ja|社殿}} (main hall) measures approximately 1.3 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and 2.0 meters in height.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|ja|伊勢津彦}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|ja|夫婦岩}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|ja|二見浦駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} of {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路}}. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:堅田神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} ; 注釈 {{Reflist|group="注"}} ; 出典 {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50年4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}} == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|かたたしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katada Shrine-qq]] [[ja:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|ja|夫婦岩}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|ja|二見浦駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} of {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路}}. revision.comment: MAINSPACE to NAMESPACE
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Katada Shrine''' (堅田神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} (sessha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It ranks 15th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 It is the shrine that initiated the production of sacred salt (御塩, goshio) offered to both the Inner and {{ill|Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja|外宮}} at {{ill|Futamiura|ja|二見浦}}, enshrining the local deity.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 77 == Overview == Katada Shrine is located at 442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.{{#tag:ref|The source notes the address as "442-2 Katada, Aza E, Futami-cho," but due to mergers with the former Ise City and other municipalities, it was changed to "442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, Ise City." {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=Ise City Citizen Exchange Division|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}|group="Note"}} It is considered one of the candidate sites (論社) for both the "Enomura Shrine" and "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The view that identifies Katada Shrine as "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" was expressed by Watari Nobunori and {{ill|Tomonobu Ban|ja|伴信友}}, while Sonoda Moriyoshi speculated that "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" might have been located west of Katada Shrine.Edited by Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 253 {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}} is also considered a candidate site for "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine." The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 2,516 square meters (2 tan, 5 se, 11 bu).Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}} (sacred object) is a stone, and the {{ill|honden|ja|社殿}} (main hall) measures approximately 1.3 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and 2.0 meters in height.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|ja|伊勢津彦}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|ja|夫婦岩}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|ja|二見浦駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} of {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路}}. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50年4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}} == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|かたたしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katada Shrine-qq]] [[ja:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:ppp]]

ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}} == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|かたたしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katada Shrine-qq]] [[ja:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:ppp]]

{{神社 |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Katada Shrine''' (堅田神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} (sessha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It ranks 15th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 It is the shrine that initiated the production of sacred salt (御塩, goshio) offered to both the Inner and {{ill|Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja|外宮}} at {{ill|Futamiura|ja|二見浦}}, enshrining the local deity.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 77 == Overview == Katada Shrine is located at 442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.{{#tag:ref|The source notes the address as "442-2 Katada, Aza E, Futami-cho," but due to mergers with the former Ise City and other municipalities, it was changed to "442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, Ise City." {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=Ise City Citizen Exchange Division|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}|group="Note"}} It is considered one of the candidate sites (論社) for both the "Enomura Shrine" and "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The view that identifies Katada Shrine as "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" was expressed by Watari Nobunori and {{ill|Tomonobu Ban|ja|伴信友}}, while Sonoda Moriyoshi speculated that "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" might have been located west of Katada Shrine.Edited by Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 253 {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}} is also considered a candidate site for "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine." The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 2,516 square meters (2 tan, 5 se, 11 bu).Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}} (sacred object) is a stone, and the {{ill|honden|ja|社殿}} (main hall) measures approximately 1.3 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and 2.0 meters in height.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|ja|伊勢津彦}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|ja|夫婦岩}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|ja|二見浦駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} of {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路}}. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50年4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. {{神社 |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Katada Shrine''' (堅田神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} (sessha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It ranks 15th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 It is the shrine that initiated the production of sacred salt (御塩, goshio) offered to both the Inner and {{ill|Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja|外宮}} at {{ill|Futamiura|ja|二見浦}}, enshrining the local deity.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 77 == Overview == Katada Shrine is located at 442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.{{#tag:ref|The source notes the address as "442-2 Katada, Aza E, Futami-cho," but due to mergers with the former Ise City and other municipalities, it was changed to "442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, Ise City." {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=Ise City Citizen Exchange Division|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}|group="Note"}} It is considered one of the candidate sites (論社) for both the "Enomura Shrine" and "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The view that identifies Katada Shrine as "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" was expressed by Watari Nobunori and {{ill|Tomonobu Ban|ja|伴信友}}, while Sonoda Moriyoshi speculated that "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" might have been located west of Katada Shrine.Edited by Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 253 {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}} is also considered a candidate site for "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine." The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 2,516 square meters (2 tan, 5 se, 11 bu).Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}} (sacred object) is a stone, and the {{ill|honden|ja|社殿}} (main hall) measures approximately 1.3 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and 2.0 meters in height.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|ja|伊勢津彦}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|ja|夫婦岩}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|ja|二見浦駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} of {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路}}. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50年4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == 関連項目 == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}} == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|かたたしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katada Shrine-qq]] [[ja:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|ja|夫婦岩}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|ja|二見浦駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} of {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路}}. revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] to [[Category:Completion tier 3]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Katada Shrine''' (堅田神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} (sessha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It ranks 15th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 It is the shrine that initiated the production of sacred salt (御塩, goshio) offered to both the Inner and {{ill|Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja|外宮}} at {{ill|Futamiura|ja|二見浦}}, enshrining the local deity.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 77 == Overview == Katada Shrine is located at 442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.{{#tag:ref|The source notes the address as "442-2 Katada, Aza E, Futami-cho," but due to mergers with the former Ise City and other municipalities, it was changed to "442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, Ise City." {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=Ise City Citizen Exchange Division|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}|group="Note"}} It is considered one of the candidate sites (論社) for both the "Enomura Shrine" and "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The view that identifies Katada Shrine as "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" was expressed by Watari Nobunori and {{ill|Tomonobu Ban|ja|伴信友}}, while Sonoda Moriyoshi speculated that "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" might have been located west of Katada Shrine.Edited by Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 253 {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}} is also considered a candidate site for "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine." The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 2,516 square meters (2 tan, 5 se, 11 bu).Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}} (sacred object) is a stone, and the {{ill|honden|ja|社殿}} (main hall) measures approximately 1.3 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and 2.0 meters in height.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|ja|伊勢津彦}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|ja|夫婦岩}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|ja|二見浦駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} of {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路}}. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50年4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}} == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|かたたしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katada Shrine-qq]] [[ja:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 3]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Categoery:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Katada Shrine''' (堅田神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} (sessha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It ranks 15th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 It is the shrine that initiated the production of sacred salt (御塩, goshio) offered to both the Inner and {{ill|Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja|外宮}} at {{ill|Futamiura|ja|二見浦}}, enshrining the local deity.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 77 == Overview == Katada Shrine is located at 442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.{{#tag:ref|The source notes the address as "442-2 Katada, Aza E, Futami-cho," but due to mergers with the former Ise City and other municipalities, it was changed to "442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, Ise City." {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=Ise City Citizen Exchange Division|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}|group="Note"}} It is considered one of the candidate sites (論社) for both the "Enomura Shrine" and "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The view that identifies Katada Shrine as "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" was expressed by Watari Nobunori and {{ill|Tomonobu Ban|ja|伴信友}}, while Sonoda Moriyoshi speculated that "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" might have been located west of Katada Shrine.Edited by Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 253 {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}} is also considered a candidate site for "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine." The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 2,516 square meters (2 tan, 5 se, 11 bu).Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}} (sacred object) is a stone, and the {{ill|honden|ja|社殿}} (main hall) measures approximately 1.3 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and 2.0 meters in height.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|ja|伊勢津彦}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|ja|夫婦岩}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|ja|二見浦駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} of {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路}}. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:堅田神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} ; 注釈 {{Reflist|group="注"}} ; 出典 {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50年4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}} == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|かたたしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Categoery:qq]] [[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katada Shrine-qq]] [[ja:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]]
