revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5th year （[[927]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Katada Shrine''' (堅田神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} (sessha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It ranks 15th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 It is the shrine that initiated the production of sacred salt (御塩, goshio) offered to both the Inner and {{ill|Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja|外宮}} at {{ill|Futamiura|ja|二見浦}}, enshrining the local deity.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 77 == Overview == Katada Shrine is located at 442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.{{#tag:ref|The source notes the address as "442-2 Katada, Aza E, Futami-cho," but due to mergers with the former Ise City and other municipalities, it was changed to "442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, Ise City." {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=Ise City Citizen Exchange Division|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}|group="Note"}} It is considered one of the candidate sites (論社) for both the "Enomura Shrine" and "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. The view that identifies Katada Shrine as "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" was expressed by Watari Nobunori and {{ill|Tomonobu Ban|ja|伴信友}}, while Sonoda Moriyoshi speculated that "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" might have been located west of Katada Shrine.Edited by Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 253 {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}} is also considered a candidate site for "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine." The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 2,516 square meters (2 tan, 5 se, 11 bu).Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}} (sacred object) is a stone, and the {{ill|honden|ja|社殿}} (main hall) measures approximately 1.3 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and 2.0 meters in height.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|ja|伊勢津彦}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|ja|夫婦岩}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|ja|二見浦駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} of {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路}}. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Katada Shrine]] [[Category:堅田神社]] [[ja:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:堅田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:堅田神社]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katada Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]]

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:堅田神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:堅田神社]] [[sn:Katada Shrine]]

revision.comment: Bot: Replaced ill templates with appended |12=draft and created corresponding redirect pages.
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5th year （[[927]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Katada Shrine''' (堅田神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社|12=draft}} (sessha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. It ranks 15th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 It is the shrine that initiated the production of sacred salt (御塩, goshio) offered to both the Inner and {{ill|Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja|外宮|12=draft}} at {{ill|Futamiura|ja|二見浦|12=draft}}, enshrining the local deity.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 77 == Overview == Katada Shrine is located at 442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}.{{#tag:ref|The source notes the address as "442-2 Katada, Aza E, Futami-cho," but due to mergers with the former Ise City and other municipalities, it was changed to "442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, Ise City." {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=Ise City Citizen Exchange Division|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}|group="Note"}} It is considered one of the candidate sites (論社) for both the "Enomura Shrine" and "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. The view that identifies Katada Shrine as "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" was expressed by Watari Nobunori and {{ill|Tomonobu Ban|ja|伴信友|12=draft}}, while Sonoda Moriyoshi speculated that "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" might have been located west of Katada Shrine.Edited by Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 253 {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=draft}} is also considered a candidate site for "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine." The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 2,516 square meters (2 tan, 5 se, 11 bu).Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|12=draft}} (sacred object) is a stone, and the {{ill|honden|ja|社殿|12=draft}} (main hall) measures approximately 1.3 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and 2.0 meters in height.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神|12=draft}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|ja|伊勢津彦|12=draft}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|ja|夫婦岩|12=draft}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|ja|二見浦駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} of {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション|12=draft}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=draft}}. == References == ; 注釈 {{Reflist|group="注"}} ; 出典 {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:堅田神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:堅田神社]] [[sn:Katada Shrine]]

{{神社 |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5th year （[[927]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Katada Shrine''' (堅田神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社|12=draft}} (sessha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. It ranks 15th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 It is the shrine that initiated the production of sacred salt (御塩, goshio) offered to both the Inner and {{ill|Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja|外宮|12=draft}} at {{ill|Futamiura|ja|二見浦|12=draft}}, enshrining the local deity.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 77 == Overview == Katada Shrine is located at 442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}.{{#tag:ref|The source notes the address as "442-2 Katada, Aza E, Futami-cho," but due to mergers with the former Ise City and other municipalities, it was changed to "442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, Ise City." {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=Ise City Citizen Exchange Division|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}|group="Note"}} It is considered one of the candidate sites (論社) for both the "Enomura Shrine" and "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. The view that identifies Katada Shrine as "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" was expressed by Watari Nobunori and {{ill|Tomonobu Ban|ja|伴信友|12=draft}}, while Sonoda Moriyoshi speculated that "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" might have been located west of Katada Shrine.Edited by Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 253 {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=draft}} is also considered a candidate site for "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine." The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 2,516 square meters (2 tan, 5 se, 11 bu).Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|12=draft}} (sacred object) is a stone, and the {{ill|honden|ja|社殿|12=draft}} (main hall) measures approximately 1.3 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and 2.0 meters in height.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神|12=draft}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|ja|伊勢津彦|12=draft}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|ja|夫婦岩|12=draft}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|ja|二見浦駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} of {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション|12=draft}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=draft}}. == References == ; 注釈 {{Reflist|group="注"}} ; 出典 {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:堅田神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:堅田神社]] [[sn:Katada Shrine]]

The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|ja|夫婦岩|12=draft|12=draft}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|ja|二見浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション|12=draft|12=draft}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == ; 注釈 {{Reflist|group="注"}} ; 出典 {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:堅田神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:堅田神社]] [[sn:Katada Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5th year （[[927]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Katada Shrine''' (堅田神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社|12=draft|12=draft}} (sessha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. It ranks 15th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 It is the shrine that initiated the production of sacred salt (御塩, goshio) offered to both the Inner and {{ill|Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja|外宮|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Futamiura|ja|二見浦|12=draft|12=draft}}, enshrining the local deity.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 77 == Overview == Katada Shrine is located at 442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{#tag:ref|The source notes the address as "442-2 Katada, Aza E, Futami-cho," but due to mergers with the former Ise City and other municipalities, it was changed to "442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, Ise City." {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=Ise City Citizen Exchange Division|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}|group="Note"}} It is considered one of the candidate sites (論社) for both the "Enomura Shrine" and "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}. The view that identifies Katada Shrine as "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" was expressed by Watari Nobunori and {{ill|Tomonobu Ban|ja|伴信友|12=draft|12=draft}}, while Sonoda Moriyoshi speculated that "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" might have been located west of Katada Shrine.Edited by Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 253 {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is also considered a candidate site for "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine." The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 2,516 square meters (2 tan, 5 se, 11 bu).Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|12=draft|12=draft}} (sacred object) is a stone, and the {{ill|honden|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}} (main hall) measures approximately 1.3 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and 2.0 meters in height.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|ja|伊勢津彦|12=draft|12=draft}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft|12=draft}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|ja|夫婦岩|12=draft|12=draft}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|ja|二見浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション|12=draft|12=draft}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == ; 注釈 {{Reflist|group="注"}} ; 出典 {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:堅田神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:堅田神社]] [[sn:Katada Shrine]]

ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:堅田神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:堅田神社]] [[sn:Katada Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5th year （[[927]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Katada Shrine''' (堅田神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社|12=draft|12=draft}} (sessha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. It ranks 15th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 It is the shrine that initiated the production of sacred salt (御塩, goshio) offered to both the Inner and {{ill|Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja|外宮|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Futamiura|ja|二見浦|12=draft|12=draft}}, enshrining the local deity.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 77 == Overview == Katada Shrine is located at 442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{#tag:ref|The source notes the address as "442-2 Katada, Aza E, Futami-cho," but due to mergers with the former Ise City and other municipalities, it was changed to "442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, Ise City." {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=Ise City Citizen Exchange Division|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}|group="Note"}} It is considered one of the candidate sites (論社) for both the "Enomura Shrine" and "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}. The view that identifies Katada Shrine as "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" was expressed by Watari Nobunori and {{ill|Tomonobu Ban|ja|伴信友|12=draft|12=draft}}, while Sonoda Moriyoshi speculated that "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" might have been located west of Katada Shrine.Edited by Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 253 {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is also considered a candidate site for "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine." The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 2,516 square meters (2 tan, 5 se, 11 bu).Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|12=draft|12=draft}} (sacred object) is a stone, and the {{ill|honden|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}} (main hall) measures approximately 1.3 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and 2.0 meters in height.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|ja|伊勢津彦|12=draft|12=draft}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft|12=draft}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|ja|夫婦岩|12=draft|12=draft}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|ja|二見浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション|12=draft|12=draft}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == ; 注釈 {{Reflist|group="注"}} ; 出典 {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:堅田神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:堅田神社]] [[sn:Katada Shrine]]

{{神社 |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5th year （[[927]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Katada Shrine''' (堅田神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社|12=draft|12=draft}} (sessha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. It ranks 15th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 It is the shrine that initiated the production of sacred salt (御塩, goshio) offered to both the Inner and {{ill|Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja|外宮|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Futamiura|ja|二見浦|12=draft|12=draft}}, enshrining the local deity.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 77 == Overview == Katada Shrine is located at 442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{#tag:ref|The source notes the address as "442-2 Katada, Aza E, Futami-cho," but due to mergers with the former Ise City and other municipalities, it was changed to "442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, Ise City." {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=Ise City Citizen Exchange Division|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}|group="Note"}} It is considered one of the candidate sites (論社) for both the "Enomura Shrine" and "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}. The view that identifies Katada Shrine as "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" was expressed by Watari Nobunori and {{ill|Tomonobu Ban|ja|伴信友|12=draft|12=draft}}, while Sonoda Moriyoshi speculated that "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" might have been located west of Katada Shrine.Edited by Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 253 {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is also considered a candidate site for "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine." The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 2,516 square meters (2 tan, 5 se, 11 bu).Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|12=draft|12=draft}} (sacred object) is a stone, and the {{ill|honden|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}} (main hall) measures approximately 1.3 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and 2.0 meters in height.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|ja|伊勢津彦|12=draft|12=draft}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft|12=draft}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|ja|夫婦岩|12=draft|12=draft}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|ja|二見浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション|12=draft|12=draft}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == ; 注釈 {{Reflist|group="注"}} ; 出典 {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories|}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:堅田神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:堅田神社]] [[sn:Katada Shrine]]

(1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|ja|伊勢津彦|12=draft|12=draft}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft|12=draft}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|ja|夫婦岩|12=draft|12=draft}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|ja|二見浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション|12=draft|12=draft}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == ; 注釈 {{Reflist|group="注"}} ; 出典 {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:堅田神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:堅田神社]] [[sn:Katada Shrine]]

{{神社 |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5th year （[[927]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Katada Shrine''' (堅田神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社|12=draft|12=draft}} (sessha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. It ranks 15th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 It is the shrine that initiated the production of sacred salt (御塩, goshio) offered to both the Inner and {{ill|Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja|外宮|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Futamiura|ja|二見浦|12=draft|12=draft}}, enshrining the local deity.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 77 == Overview == Katada Shrine is located at 442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{#tag:ref|The source notes the address as "442-2 Katada, Aza E, Futami-cho," but due to mergers with the former Ise City and other municipalities, it was changed to "442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, Ise City." {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=Ise City Citizen Exchange Division|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}|group="Note"}} It is considered one of the candidate sites (論社) for both the "Enomura Shrine" and "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}. The view that identifies Katada Shrine as "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" was expressed by Watari Nobunori and {{ill|Tomonobu Ban|ja|伴信友|12=draft|12=draft}}, while Sonoda Moriyoshi speculated that "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" might have been located west of Katada Shrine.Edited by Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 253 {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is also considered a candidate site for "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine." The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 2,516 square meters (2 tan, 5 se, 11 bu).Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|12=draft|12=draft}} (sacred object) is a stone, and the {{ill|honden|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}} (main hall) measures approximately 1.3 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and 2.0 meters in height.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|ja|伊勢津彦|12=draft|12=draft}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft|12=draft}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|ja|夫婦岩|12=draft|12=draft}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|ja|二見浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション|12=draft|12=draft}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == ; 注釈 {{Reflist|group="注"}} ; 出典 {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:堅田神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:堅田神社]] [[sn:Katada Shrine]]

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:堅田神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:堅田神社]] [[sn:Katada Shrine]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Return the see also]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5th year （[[927]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Katada Shrine''' (堅田神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社|12=draft|12=draft}} (sessha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. It ranks 15th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 It is the shrine that initiated the production of sacred salt (御塩, goshio) offered to both the Inner and {{ill|Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja|外宮|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Futamiura|ja|二見浦|12=draft|12=draft}}, enshrining the local deity.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 77 == Overview == Katada Shrine is located at 442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{#tag:ref|The source notes the address as "442-2 Katada, Aza E, Futami-cho," but due to mergers with the former Ise City and other municipalities, it was changed to "442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, Ise City." {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=Ise City Citizen Exchange Division|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}|group="Note"}} It is considered one of the candidate sites (論社) for both the "Enomura Shrine" and "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}. The view that identifies Katada Shrine as "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" was expressed by Watari Nobunori and {{ill|Tomonobu Ban|ja|伴信友|12=draft|12=draft}}, while Sonoda Moriyoshi speculated that "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" might have been located west of Katada Shrine.Edited by Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 253 {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is also considered a candidate site for "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine." The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 2,516 square meters (2 tan, 5 se, 11 bu).Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|12=draft|12=draft}} (sacred object) is a stone, and the {{ill|honden|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}} (main hall) measures approximately 1.3 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and 2.0 meters in height.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|ja|伊勢津彦|12=draft|12=draft}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft|12=draft}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|ja|夫婦岩|12=draft|12=draft}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|ja|二見浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション|12=draft|12=draft}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == ; 注釈 {{Reflist|group="注"}} ; 出典 {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:堅田神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:堅田神社]] [[sn:Katada Shrine]]

{{神社 |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5th year （[[927]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Katada Shrine''' (堅田神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社|12=draft|12=draft}} (sessha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. It ranks 15th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 It is the shrine that initiated the production of sacred salt (御塩, goshio) offered to both the Inner and {{ill|Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja|外宮|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Futamiura|ja|二見浦|12=draft|12=draft}}, enshrining the local deity.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 77 == Overview == Katada Shrine is located at 442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{#tag:ref|The source notes the address as "442-2 Katada, Aza E, Futami-cho," but due to mergers with the former Ise City and other municipalities, it was changed to "442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, Ise City." {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=Ise City Citizen Exchange Division|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}|group="Note"}} It is considered one of the candidate sites (論社) for both the "Enomura Shrine" and "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}. The view that identifies Katada Shrine as "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" was expressed by Watari Nobunori and {{ill|Tomonobu Ban|ja|伴信友|12=draft|12=draft}}, while Sonoda Moriyoshi speculated that "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" might have been located west of Katada Shrine.Edited by Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 253 {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is also considered a candidate site for "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine." The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 2,516 square meters (2 tan, 5 se, 11 bu).Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai|12=draft|12=draft}} (sacred object) is a stone, and the {{ill|honden|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}} (main hall) measures approximately 1.3 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and 2.0 meters in height.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|ja|伊勢津彦|12=draft|12=draft}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft|12=draft}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|ja|二見興玉神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|ja|夫婦岩|12=draft|12=draft}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|ja|二見浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション|12=draft|12=draft}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == ; 注釈 {{Reflist|group="注"}} ; 出典 {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:堅田神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:二見町]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:堅田神社]] [[sn:Katada Shrine]]
