revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5th year （[[927]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.505667|N|136.779833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Katada Shrine''' (堅田神社) is a {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|WD=Q1071160|lt=branch shrine|1=Setsumatsusha}} (sessha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It Ranks 15th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 It is the shrine that initiated the production of sacred salt (御塩, goshio) offered to both the Inner and {{ill|Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633343}} at {{ill|Futami-ga-ura|en|Futami-ga-ura|WD=Q11371796|lt=Futamiura|1=Futami-ga-ura, Mie}}, enshrining the local deity.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 77 == Overview == Katada Shrine is located at 442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.{{#tag:ref|The source notes the address as "442-2 Katada, Aza E, Futami-cho," but due to mergers with the former Ise City and other municipalities, it was changed to "442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, Ise City." {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=Ise City Citizen Exchange Division|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}|group="Note"}} It is considered one of the candidate sites (論社) for both the "Enomura Shrine" and "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The view that identifies Katada Shrine as "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" was expressed by Watari Nobunori and {{ill|Tomonobu Ban|ja|伴信友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonobu Ban|1=Ban Nobutomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346747}}, while Sonoda Moriyoshi speculated that "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" might have been located west of Katada Shrine.Edited by Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 253 {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}} is also considered a candidate site for "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine." The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 2,516 square meters (2 tan, 5 se, 11 bu).Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The {{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|WD=Q327532|lt=Shintai|1=Shintai}} (sacred object) is a stone, and the {{ill|honden|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/honden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築}} (main hall) measures approximately 1.3 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and 2.0 meters in height.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|en|Isetsuhiko|WD=Q17193127|lt=Isetsuhiko|1=Isetsuhiko}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|en|Futami Okitama Shrine|WD=Q64969614|lt=Futami Okitama Shrine|1=Futami Okitama Shrine}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|en|Meoto Iwa|WD=Q333875|lt=Meoto Iwa|1=Meoto Iwa}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|en|Futaminoura Station|WD=Q117376|lt=Futaminoura Station|1=Futaminoura Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} of {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futami Junction|1=Futami junction|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10880410}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378935}}. == References == ; Note {{Reflist|group="注"}} ; References {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University|1=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[:en:小学館|小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishioden Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|堅田神社|version=97959843|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Katada Shrine (Q17228121)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! {{Infobox religious building |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5th year （[[927]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.505667|N|136.779833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Katada Shrine''' (堅田神社) is a {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|qid=Q1071160|lt=branch shrine|1=Setsumatsusha}} (sessha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It Ranks 15th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 It is the shrine that initiated the production of sacred salt (御塩, goshio) offered to both the Inner and {{ill|Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11633343}} at {{ill|Futami-ga-ura|en|Futami-ga-ura|qid=Q11371796|lt=Futamiura|1=Futami-ga-ura, Mie}}, enshrining the local deity.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 77 == Overview == Katada Shrine is located at 442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.{{#tag:ref|The source notes the address as "442-2 Katada, Aza E, Futami-cho," but due to mergers with the former Ise City and other municipalities, it was changed to "442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, Ise City." {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=Ise City Citizen Exchange Division|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}|group="Note"}} It is considered one of the candidate sites (論社) for both the "Enomura Shrine" and "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The view that identifies Katada Shrine as "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" was expressed by Watari Nobunori and {{ill|Tomonobu Ban|ja|伴信友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonobu Ban|1=Ban Nobutomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4346747}}, while Sonoda Moriyoshi speculated that "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" might have been located west of Katada Shrine.Edited by Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 253 {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}} is also considered a candidate site for "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine." The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 2,516 square meters (2 tan, 5 se, 11 bu).Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The {{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|qid=Q327532|lt=Shintai|1=Shintai}} (sacred object) is a stone, and the {{ill|honden|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/honden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築}} (main hall) measures approximately 1.3 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and 2.0 meters in height.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|en|Isetsuhiko|qid=Q17193127|lt=Isetsuhiko|1=Isetsuhiko}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|qid=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qid=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|en|Futami Okitama Shrine|qid=Q64969614|lt=Futami Okitama Shrine|1=Futami Okitama Shrine}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|en|Meoto Iwa|qid=Q333875|lt=Meoto Iwa|1=Meoto Iwa}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|en|Futaminoura Station|qid=Q117376|lt=Futaminoura Station|1=Futaminoura Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|qid=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} of {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|qid=Q513679|lt=JR Central|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futami Junction|1=Futami junction|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10880410}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11378935}}. == References == ; Note {{Reflist|group="注"}} ; References {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|qid=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University|1=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[:en:小学館|小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishioden Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|堅田神社|version=97959843|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Katada Shrine (Q17228121)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Katada Shrine|lt=Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|qid=Q17228121|1=Katada Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || — || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|qid=Q11581011|1=Kōtai Jingū}} || {{ill|Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|lt=Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|qid=Q135039265|1=Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine}}; {{ill|Wemurano Shrine|lt=Wemurano Shrine|qid=Q135039268|1=Wemurano Shrine}}; {{ill|Wemurano Shrine|lt=Wemurano Shrine|qid=Q135039268|1=Wemurano Shrine}} || {{coord|34.505698|136.779849|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181129 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17228121}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17228121]] [[ja:堅田神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Futami Town]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 100+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 150+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 200+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 250+ untranslated japanese characters]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5th year （[[927]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.505667|N|136.779833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Katada Shrine''' (堅田神社) is a {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|qid=Q1071160|lt=branch shrine|1=Setsumatsusha}} (sessha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It Ranks 15th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 It is the shrine that initiated the production of sacred salt (御塩, goshio) offered to both the Inner and {{ill|Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11633343}} at {{ill|Futami-ga-ura|en|Futami-ga-ura|qid=Q11371796|lt=Futamiura|1=Futami-ga-ura, Mie}}, enshrining the local deity.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 77 == Overview == Katada Shrine is located at 442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.{{#tag:ref|The source notes the address as "442-2 Katada, Aza E, Futami-cho," but due to mergers with the former Ise City and other municipalities, it was changed to "442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, Ise City." {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=Ise City Citizen Exchange Division|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}|group="Note"}} It is considered one of the candidate sites (論社) for both the "Enomura Shrine" and "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The view that identifies Katada Shrine as "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" was expressed by Watari Nobunori and {{ill|Tomonobu Ban|ja|伴信友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonobu Ban|1=Ban Nobutomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4346747}}, while Sonoda Moriyoshi speculated that "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" might have been located west of Katada Shrine.Edited by Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 253 {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}} is also considered a candidate site for "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine." The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 2,516 square meters (2 tan, 5 se, 11 bu).Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The {{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|qid=Q327532|lt=Shintai|1=Shintai}} (sacred object) is a stone, and the {{ill|honden|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/honden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築}} (main hall) measures approximately 1.3 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and 2.0 meters in height.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|en|Isetsuhiko|qid=Q17193127|lt=Isetsuhiko|1=Isetsuhiko}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|qid=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qid=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|en|Futami Okitama Shrine|qid=Q64969614|lt=Futami Okitama Shrine|1=Futami Okitama Shrine}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|en|Meoto Iwa|qid=Q333875|lt=Meoto Iwa|1=Meoto Iwa}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|en|Futaminoura Station|qid=Q117376|lt=Futaminoura Station|1=Futaminoura Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|qid=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} of {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|qid=Q513679|lt=JR Central|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futami Junction|1=Futami junction|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10880410}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11378935}}. == References == ; Note {{Reflist|group="注"}} ; References {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|qid=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University|1=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[:en:小学館|小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Mishioden Shrine|ja|御塩殿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishioden Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area10/area10_01.jpg 堅田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m10/1002.html 堅田神社（かただじんじゃ）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|堅田神社|version=97959843|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Katada Shrine (Q17228121)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Katada Shrine|lt=Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|qid=Q17228121|1=Katada Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || — || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|qid=Q11581011|1=Kōtai Jingū}} || {{ill|Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|lt=Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|qid=Q135039265|1=Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine}}; {{ill|Wemurano Shrine|lt=Wemurano Shrine|qid=Q135039268|1=Wemurano Shrine}}; {{ill|Wemurano Shrine|lt=Wemurano Shrine|qid=Q135039268|1=Wemurano Shrine}} || {{coord|34.505698|136.779849|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181129 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17228121}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17228121]] [[ja:堅田神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Futami Town]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 100+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 150+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 200+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 250+ untranslated japanese characters]]

{{Infobox religious building |名称= 堅田神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Katada-jinnja (Naiku) 01.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]二見町茶屋[[小字|字]]堅田442-2 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 20.4 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 46 | 経度秒 = 47.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 佐見都日女命 |社格= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）論社
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社 |創建= [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5th year （[[927]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.505667|N|136.779833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Katada Shrine''' (堅田神社) is a {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|qid=Q1071160|lt=branch shrine|1=Setsumatsusha}} (sessha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It Ranks 15th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9-11 It is the shrine that initiated the production of sacred salt (御塩, goshio) offered to both the Inner and {{ill|Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine (Ise)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11633343}} at {{ill|Futami-ga-ura|en|Futami-ga-ura|qid=Q11371796|lt=Futamiura|1=Futami-ga-ura, Mie}}, enshrining the local deity.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 77 == Overview == Katada Shrine is located at 442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.{{#tag:ref|The source notes the address as "442-2 Katada, Aza E, Futami-cho," but due to mergers with the former Ise City and other municipalities, it was changed to "442-2 Katada, Aza Chaya, Futami-cho, Ise City." {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|title=町の区域の設定調書|publisher=Ise City Citizen Exchange Division|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608120807/http://www.city.ise.mie.jp/secure/4736/setteityousyo.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}}|group="Note"}} It is considered one of the candidate sites (論社) for both the "Enomura Shrine" and "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. The view that identifies Katada Shrine as "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" was expressed by Watari Nobunori and {{ill|Tomonobu Ban|ja|伴信友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonobu Ban|1=Ban Nobutomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4346747}}, while Sonoda Moriyoshi speculated that "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" might have been located west of Katada Shrine.Edited by Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p. 253 {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}} is also considered a candidate site for "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine." The shrine grounds cover an area of approximately 2,516 square meters (2 tan, 5 se, 11 bu).Edited by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The {{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|qid=Q327532|lt=Shintai|1=Shintai}} (sacred object) is a stone, and the {{ill|honden|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/honden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築}} (main hall) measures approximately 1.3 meters in length, 1.5 meters in width, and 2.0 meters in height.Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 == Enshrined Deity == The {{ill|kami|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} enshrined is Samitsu-hime no Mikoto (佐見都日女命).Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 95Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 70 She is considered a local deity. If Katada Shrine is regarded as the successor to the ancient "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine," Samitsu-hime no Mikoto would be the daughter of {{ill|Isetsuhiko|en|Isetsuhiko|qid=Q17193127|lt=Isetsuhiko|1=Isetsuhiko}}, the great land deity of {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|qid=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}. The name "Samitsu" refers to an old place name of the area. == History == When {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qid=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} visited Futami during her journey through the provinces, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto, the local deity, presented her with hard salt (堅塩, katajio). Yamatohime-no-mikoto was pleased and established Katada Shrine. However, Samitsu-hime no Mikoto did not offer the salt willingly; instead, she silently presented it without answering Yamatohime-no-mikoto’s questions, as a form of resistance to giving up her cultivated land. This event marked the origin of sacred salt production at Futamiura for offerings to the Inner and Outer Shrines. Although the shrine's rituals were interrupted for a time, Arakida Ujiyuki revived them in 1434 (Eikyō 6). When Futami became shrine territory again in 1670 (Kanbun 10), the {{ill|Edo shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ordered the local people to perform festivals and shrine rebuilding, which they did with great dedication.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|title=Course 05 Futami Route – Visiting Shrines, Meoto Iwa, and Somin’s Forest in the Land of Purification and Salt|publisher=Ise City Tourism Association|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131026232640/http://www.isekanko.jp/k-cycling/course05.html|archivedate=2013-10-26}} The shrine was rebuilt in March 1921 (Taishō 10). == Surroundings == Katada Shrine is located at the western foot of Mt. Otonashi, directly across from the Futami General Branch Office of Ise City Hall. Nearby is the Futami inn district, and passing through it leads to {{ill|Futami Okitama Shrine|en|Futami Okitama Shrine|qid=Q64969614|lt=Futami Okitama Shrine|1=Futami Okitama Shrine}} and {{ill|Meoto Iwa|en|Meoto Iwa|qid=Q333875|lt=Meoto Iwa|1=Meoto Iwa}} (the "Wedded Rocks"). It’s about a 6-minute walk (approximately 400 meters) from {{ill|Futaminoura Station|en|Futaminoura Station|qid=Q117376|lt=Futaminoura Station|1=Futaminoura Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|qid=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} of {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|qid=Q513679|lt=JR Central|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}.{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|title=Route 13: The Sacred Salt Road|publisher=Yokoyama Visitor Center|accessdate=2014-06-08|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608131007/http://www.yokoyama-vc.jp/marugoto/walking60/13.pdf|archivedate=2014-06-08}} It is also right in front of the Futami General Branch Office bus stop. By car, it is about a 1-minute (1.2 km) drive from {{ill|Futami Junction|ja|二見ジャンクション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futami Junction|1=Futami junction|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10880410}} via National Route 42 after exiting the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽有料道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Toll Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11378935}}. == References == ; Note {{Reflist|group="注"}} ; References {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p.
